speaker: Course tutor
en: Oh, Karen.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Hi, come in, take a seat.

speaker: Course tutor
en: I wanted to talk to you about this assignment you've handed in.

speaker: Course tutor
en: I'll give it back to you with detailed feedback, but first there are a few things I'd like to run over.

speaker: Course tutor
en: You know pointers for next time.

speaker: Course tutor
en: When you hand in your next assignment, can you ensure that you've gone through these aspects?

speaker: Karen
en: Hum, sure, will do.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Now, the first thing is your literature review.

speaker: Course tutor
en: It was not sufficient enough so I would like to see your book reports to get your complete view.

speaker: Course tutor
en: So can you submit those with the next assignment?

speaker: Course tutor
en: Then I can find out which parts you've chosen to leave out.

speaker: Karen
en: Oh, yes.

speaker: Karen
en: Sorry, I didn't realise you needed them.

speaker: Karen
en: It did cross my mind that what I've handed in is a bit thin, but then I started to work on another project and forgot to revise it.

speaker: Course tutor
en: That's alright.

speaker: Course tutor
en: And I've found some errors, just small ones where you had quoted people but not recorded the information properly at the end.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Don't forget to go through and make sure that your references are accurate.

speaker: Course tutor
en: They were very relevant, though.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Just check and revise them using the format sample that I've emailed you beforehand, OK?

speaker: Karen
en: Yes, I'll remember to check them.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Now, you made some good points but it might be helpful if you could include a few other examples, just to drive your point home.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Don't start writing more paragraphs; just slot them in at the end of what you've already written.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Now the thing you have to do, if you really want to get a better mark, is expand the ideas you're presenting.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Then your argument becomes more valid.

speaker: Karen
en: OK.

speaker: Karen
en: Hum, can I talk to you about this presentation I've got to do?

speaker: Course tutor
en: Sure, of course.

speaker: Karen
en: When shall I stage what we've studied?

speaker: Karen
en: Am I doing it next term?

speaker: Karen
en: I can't remember what we said.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Well, the thing is, Marco couldn't do it, so you agreed to do it at the next seminar.

speaker: Course tutor
en: So how can I help?

speaker: Karen
en: Um … actually, I'm wondering what you suggest to be the key part of the presentation.

speaker: Karen
en: I mean, which part should I spend most of my time on?

speaker: Course tutor
en: That's a question to the point.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Well, you have very little time really, so it's absolutely essential for you to explain the experiment.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Of course, you'll have a summary in the handouts you give out but you can still start the presentation with that.

speaker: Course tutor
en: It's optional, though.

speaker: Karen
en: And do I have to give you the abstract first, or shall I just email it to all the students?

speaker: Course tutor
en: Uh, no.

speaker: Course tutor
en: I do need to see it first and see if it needs to be polished up.

speaker: Course tutor
en: And then we'll get some printouts done.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Now, they'll need to be done by 3rd December, so I'll need to see it by 26th November if that's OK?

speaker: Karen
en: Yes, fine.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Oh, and I need to talk to you about where the presentation is going to be made.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Given the actual number of participants and audience, we've had problems with the rooms because we'll need something bigger than usual.

speaker: Course tutor
en: In our faculty, the only room available is the computer room, which is far from suitable considering the layout of the room, so we'll have to go across the road and do it in the chemistry lab.

speaker: Course tutor
en: It's big enough to accommodate everyone and is equipped with the proper overhead projector in there as well.

speaker: Karen
en: OK, right.

speaker: Karen
en: And I get a grade for this, don't I?

speaker: Course tutor
en: Yes, your first one was graded by your tutor, but this one will be scored by the professor.

speaker: Course tutor
en: But relax, the criteria will be consistent, and you'll get an unbiased result.

speaker: Karen
en: Oh, and I've sorted out my modules for next year.

speaker: Karen
en: You asked me to tell you about my choices.

speaker: Course tutor
en: Yes, what've you decided?

speaker: Karen
en: Oh, it was really hard to determine.

speaker: Karen
en: Hum, I've already done the Data Collection once, so that wasn't really a choice.

speaker: Karen
en: I couldn't make up my mind between Language and Society and Communication Skills.

speaker: Karen
en: I read the syllabuses but they sound more or less the same to me.

speaker: Karen
en: Anyway, I went for Communication Skills in the end because I know the lecturer.

speaker: Karen
en: Actually, Social Interaction seems to cover much the same ground, so I didn't bother with that either.

speaker: Karen
en: Hum, I thought Discourse Analysis looked really interesting.

speaker: Karen
en: It studies and analyses the texts beyond the literal meaning of the sentences.

speaker: Karen
en: It looks into the connotation beyond the sentences.

speaker: Karen
en: And in fact, it also covers a little bit of Research Methodology in it, so I thought I'd do that, rather than the full methodology course.

speaker: Karen
en: Kill two birds with one stone as it were.

speaker: Karen
en: And then I fancied something drastically different, so I thought Psycholinguistics would be interesting, unless you think it'll be more worthwhile for me to do the Phonology course?

speaker: Course tutor
en: No, I think you've made sensible choices.

speaker: Course tutor
en: I'm glad you're organised.

speaker: Course tutor
en: OK, let's meet again in a couple of weeks to see how you're getting on.

speaker: Karen
en: OK, thank you.

speaker: Karen
en: See you then.
